_note: Updated daily at noon by the marketing agent. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Regular posts showing how the tools and game are being built
priorities.note: Building in public creates a searchable history and earns trust before we ask anyone to pay for anything. Each post should show real progress, not announcements.

priorities.title: Getting the AI messaging right — the human is always in control
priorities.note: There's a backlash against tools that oversell AI automation. Our message needs to be precise: the AI suggests, you decide. Lead with what it does for your workflow, not what it does on its own.

priorities.title: Short screen recording demos — these work better than any written description
priorities.note: A 15 to 60 second recording of the tool in action beats any amount of copy for convincing another developer to try it.

priorities.title: One-page update every Tuesday
priorities.note: Links, engagement numbers, anything interesting spotted from competitors, and what's planned for the coming week.

priorities.title: Keeping an eye on what similar tools are doing
priorities.note: Watching Tiled, LDtk, and anything new adding AI to level design. The gap between us and them is we have AI and a full pipeline from sprite to room to world. That's the message.

metrics.channels_cadence: itch.io devlog and X — aiming for 3 to 5 posts a week
metrics.competitor_watch: Quarterly — Tiled, LDtk, and any AI level-design tools

actions.name: Content Brief
actions.description: Draft a devlog or social post from bullet-point notes — founder-authentic voice, not corporate.
actions.trigger: A feature ships or a milestone is reached that warrants public communication

actions.name: Competitor Monitor
actions.description: Competitive landscape update: new entrants, community sentiment shifts, AI game tool developments.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or when a new entrant is flagged

actions.name: Launch Checklist
actions.description: Messaging readiness assessment, channel plan, and risks to authenticity or accuracy for a feature release.
actions.trigger: Before any feature or product release

actions.name: Positioning Review
actions.description: Accuracy check on AI feature claims or marketing copy — flag over-claims, rewrite where needed.
actions.trigger: Any AI feature claim, marketing copy, or public announcement draft
